sentence_reviews.text: Relevant records, documents, examples, or tools are selected from an external collection.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA supports broad information retrieval/search concepts; Deep Learning supports learned representation background.
sentence_reviews.notes: The cited source set directly supports retrieval as selecting relevant information from a collection.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of External retrieval & ranking is to connect Databases & information retrieval, Embeddings & distributed representations to downstream capabilities including Episodic memory, Sparse lexical retrieval, Dense vector retrieval.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The packet sources support IR, representation, and ranking background, but not the entire downstream atlas list.
sentence_reviews.notes: Sparse and dense retrieval are defensible; episodic memory as downstream capability needs a more specific source.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The cited sources provide broad memory/retrieval background but not this exact AI memory-system relationship.
sentence_reviews.notes: This needs a source on episodic memory systems or retrieval-augmented memory.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA supports broad information retrieval and ranking concepts.
sentence_reviews.notes: Sparse lexical retrieval is a direct kind of external retrieval, but a dedicated IR reference would be stronger.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The cited sources support distributed representations and probabilistic/representation learning background.
sentence_reviews.notes: Dense vector retrieval relies on embeddings plus retrieval, but direct evidence would require a dense retrieval source.

sentence_reviews.text: Its benefit depends on implementation, scale, data, and hardware; the same mechanism can add complexity without improving the target workload. Its direct dependencies include Databases & information retrieval, Embeddings & distributed representations.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The cited sources support dependency background, but not the generic limitation statement.
sentence_reviews.notes: The dependencies are plausible; the limitation needs direct retrieval-system evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: External retrieval & ranking uses Databases & information retrieval as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA supports information retrieval and search over external information sources.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is essentially definitional, but the packet source is broad.

sentence_reviews.text: External retrieval & ranking uses Embeddings & distributed representations as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The cited sources support learned representations and embeddings.
sentence_reviews.notes: Embeddings are common for dense retrieval and ranking, but not required for all external retrieval.

sentence_reviews.text: External retrieval & ranking uses Datasets, sampling & splits as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML supports dataset and sampling background, not direct retrieval/ranking operation.
sentence_reviews.notes: External collections can be datasets, but sampling/splits are not clearly part of retrieval's defined operation.

sentence_reviews.text: External retrieval & ranking uses Margin, ranking & metric objectives as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML provides broad objective and metric-learning background relevant to ranking.
sentence_reviews.notes: Ranking/metric objectives are common in learned retrieval, but not universal across all external retrieval.

edge_reviews.notes: External retrieval directly uses information retrieval concepts; core strength is defensible.
Embeddings are common for dense retrieval; common strength is defensible.
External collections may be datasets, but sampling/splits are not clearly a mechanism dependency.
Ranking and metric objectives are common for learned retrieval and ranking systems.
